caveat: This framework content pack is a structured starter representation and does not constitute legal advice or complete regulatory coverage. This is a starter content pack for scoping purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice or a complete compliance determination.

obligations.title: Prohibit disallowed AI practices
obligations.description: Ensure no deployment of prohibited AI practices within the organization's products or operations, including practices prohibited by Article 5.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Do not place on the market, put into service, or use AI practices prohibited by Article 5.
obligations.applicability_notes: Baseline obligation for all EU-in-scope deployments.

obligations.title: Operate a lifecycle risk management system for high-risk AI
obligations.description: Establish, implement, document, and maintain a continuous risk management system for each high-risk AI system. The process should identify known and reasonably foreseeable risks to health, safety, and fundamental rights; estimate and evaluate those risks, including reasonably foreseeable misuse; adopt risk-control measures; and use post-market monitoring evidence to keep the assessment current.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Continuously identify, evaluate, mitigate, and monitor risks from high-risk AI systems.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to high-risk AI systems under Chapter III, Section 2.

obligations.title: Apply data governance and quality controls
obligations.description: For high-risk AI systems using data for training, validation, or testing, maintain data governance and management practices covering design choices, data collection, preparation, relevance, representativeness, accuracy, completeness, bias detection and mitigation, data gaps, and suitability for the intended purpose.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Use governed, suitable, representative, and bias-managed data for high-risk AI.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies where high-risk AI systems use training, validation, or testing datasets.

obligations.title: Maintain technical documentation before placing on market or putting into service
obligations.description: Prepare and keep up-to-date technical documentation demonstrating that the high-risk AI system complies with EU AI Act requirements. Documentation should enable competent authorities and conformity assessors to understand the system's purpose, design, development, operation, risk controls, monitoring, and changes.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Keep technical documentation sufficient to demonstrate high-risk AI compliance.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies before placing on the market or putting high-risk AI into service and after significant changes.

obligations.title: Enable automatic record-keeping and event logs
obligations.description: Design high-risk AI systems with logging capabilities that automatically record events over the system lifecycle where technically feasible. Logs should support traceability of system functioning, monitoring, incident analysis, and post-market oversight proportionate to the intended purpose and risk profile.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Generate and retain logs that support traceability and oversight of high-risk AI.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to high-risk AI system design and operation where logging is technically feasible.

obligations.title: Provide transparency and instructions for deployers
obligations.description: Design and accompany high-risk AI systems with clear, concise, complete, and correct instructions for use. The information should allow deployers to understand the provider, system characteristics, intended purpose, performance, limitations, human oversight measures, input data expectations, and maintenance needs.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Give deployers the information needed to use high-risk AI safely and appropriately.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to high-risk AI system information supplied to deployers and users.

obligations.title: Implement effective human oversight
obligations.description: Design high-risk AI systems so natural persons can effectively oversee them during use, understand capabilities and limitations, monitor operation, interpret outputs appropriately, decide not to use or override outputs, and intervene or stop the system where necessary to prevent or minimise risks.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Ensure trained people can monitor, understand, override, and stop high-risk AI when needed.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to oversight measures built into or implemented around high-risk AI systems.

obligations.title: Meet accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity requirements
obligations.description: Design and develop high-risk AI systems to achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity throughout their lifecycle. Providers should declare relevant accuracy metrics, protect against errors, faults, inconsistencies, unauthorised access, data poisoning, model manipulation, and other adversarial or security threats proportionate to the system's risks.
obligations.plain_language_summary: Set and maintain lifecycle accuracy, robustness, resilience, and cybersecurity controls.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to high-risk AI system design, validation, operation, and lifecycle monitoring.

obligations.title: Complete conformity assessment and registration
obligations.description: Perform required conformity assessment and system registration before placing on market where applicable.
obligations.applicability_notes: Check routes and deadlines for CE marking and registration obligations.

obligations.title: Operate post-market monitoring and incident reporting
obligations.description: Implement post-market monitoring and serious-incident reporting processes.
obligations.applicability_notes: Incident triage and escalation criteria should be defined.

obligations.title: Provide user-facing transparency notices
obligations.description: Where required, inform individuals that they are interacting with AI or exposed to AI-generated or manipulated content.
obligations.applicability_notes: Applies to applicable transparency use cases under Article 50.

content_versions.obligation_text: Do not place or use prohibited AI practices listed in Article 5.
Operate a documented, continuous risk management system for high-risk AI systems covering identification, analysis, evaluation, mitigation, testing, and post-market feedback.
Apply data governance and quality controls for training, validation, and testing datasets used by high-risk AI systems.
Maintain technical documentation that demonstrates conformity and allows authorities and assessors to understand the system and its controls.
Enable automatic record-keeping and logs for traceability, monitoring, incident analysis, and post-market oversight.
Provide deployers with clear instructions for use, including intended purpose, performance, limitations, oversight, input data, and maintenance information.
Implement human oversight measures enabling trained people to monitor, interpret, override, intervene, or stop high-risk AI systems where necessary.
Design and maintain high-risk AI systems for appropriate accuracy, robustness, resilience, and cybersecurity throughout the lifecycle.
Complete required conformity assessment and registrations.
Run post-market monitoring and incident reporting workflows.
Provide required transparency notices to affected individuals.

evidence_requirements.title: High-risk AI risk management record
evidence_requirements.description: Documented lifecycle risk management process, risk register, mitigation decisions, test results, and post-market review inputs.
evidence_requirements.frequency: At initial release and after material changes

evidence_requirements.title: Data governance and quality evidence
evidence_requirements.description: Dataset provenance, suitability, representativeness, quality, bias assessment, and remediation records for training, validation, or testing data.
evidence_requirements.frequency: Per model version or material dataset change

evidence_requirements.title: Technical documentation package
evidence_requirements.description: Current technical documentation covering intended purpose, design, development, operation, controls, monitoring, and changes.
evidence_requirements.frequency: Current before release and after significant changes

evidence_requirements.title: Logging and traceability evidence
evidence_requirements.description: Configuration or system export showing event logging, retention, traceability, and review process for high-risk AI operation.

evidence_requirements.title: Instructions for use
evidence_requirements.description: Deployers receive clear instructions covering intended purpose, performance, limitations, oversight, input data, and maintenance requirements.
evidence_requirements.frequency: Current before deployment and after material changes

evidence_requirements.title: Human oversight design and operating evidence
evidence_requirements.description: Oversight procedure, operator training, escalation paths, intervention/override controls, and stop-use criteria.

evidence_requirements.title: Accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity testing
evidence_requirements.description: Validation metrics, robustness testing, security testing, adversarial threat review, and lifecycle monitoring evidence.
